task_completion.description: Select the bone tissue threshold to create a mask
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the InVesalius thresholding panel, the 'Bone' preset is selected, or the threshold values are set to capture bone (approximately 226 to 3071 HU), and the bone structure is highlighted in the 2D slice views.

task_completion.description: Generate the 3D surface of the bone tissue
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D model of a skull/cranium is clearly visible in the 3D viewer pane of the InVesalius interface.
A file manager dialog for exporting or saving the 3D surface is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Set the correct export directory and filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the save dialog, the current directory is set to 'Documents' (or /home/ga/Documents) and the file name field contains 'cranial_bone'.
In the save dialog, the file type/format dropdown is explicitly set to 'VTK PolyData (*.vtp)' or similar, ensuring it is not STL or OBJ.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius GUI to generate and export the surface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the InVesalius application menus, thresholding tools, and 3D viewer, rather than just terminal windows.

integrity.description: The generated 3D surface is genuinely a cranium
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D model in the viewer actually resembles a human skull, indicating the correct dataset and threshold were processed.

integrity.description: The agent did not mock the file creation using terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal commands like 'touch /home/ga/Documents/cranial_bone.vtp' or echo commands used to create a fake, empty file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target output path is exactly /home/ga/Documents/cranial_bone.vtp. The expected 3D surface should represent a cranial bone (skull). A genuine export will result in a file significantly larger than 100 KB (typically ~18 MB) with over 5,000 points and 10,000 polygons. The correct bone threshold range in InVesalius is approximately 226 to 3071 HU.
